notes:
Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only visible evidence is ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, case_packet, 4971 bytes, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17. Packet proves the rule, market identity, capture time, and operative elements: Iran-initiated, non-weather, by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET, major closure. Major closure can be broad commercial-flight closure/suspension across Iran or a major Iranian airspace region, or non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Packet does not itself prove a May 22-24 closure occurred.

Native investigation before opening: searched web for May 24 2026 Iran airspace closure, Reuters, CAA/IRNA, and NOTAM/western Tehran FIR terms. Leads, not record evidence: SafeAirspace/OpsGroup describe Tehran FIR/OIIX restrictions and partial reopening; secondary reports/social snippets point to an Iranian CAA NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 effective May 22-May 25 closing western airspace/western airports. Need later capture/admission of primary NOTAM or official Iranian aviation source and corroborating credible reporting before relying on those facts. Adverse issues: foreign airline warnings/avoidance do not qualify; partial western-sector closure must be shown to be a major region or to affect at least two listed airports; weather exclusion likely not central but must be proven non-weather/security rather than weather. Opening will frame those decisive issues without asserting unadmitted source facts as established.

Turn: openings:defendant. Objective: defendant opening on whether by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace under market rule. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence/stat/read show only case-packet evidence ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, 4971 bytes text/plain. Packet rule requires Iran as initiator, non-weather, by deadline, and major closure: broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing Iranian airspace or major Iranian airspace region, or complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN. Partial/local closures, foreign warnings, airline/country restrictions, and weather do not qualify. Primary resolution sources: official Iranian aviation authorities and consensus credible reporting.

Investigation/search log: searched web for May 24 2026 Iran airspace NOTAM, Tehran FIR, official CAA, Reuters, and exact NOTAM terms OIIX A1010/26. Leads found: Notamify page for OIIX A1010/26 displayed raw NOTAM text: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW operating sunrise to sunset; prior permissions suspended; new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger operations; B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST. Indian Express reported Iranian Civil Aviation Authority issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 closing western Tehran FIR airports May 22-May 25 with limited daylight permissions, citing Gulf News/source chain. SafeAirspace Iran page says June 2026 eastern OIIX partially reopened while western remains closed; EASA CZIB gives conflict-risk context and foreign operator recommendations but not Iran-initiated closure proof. Adverse/provenance issue: quick search found snippets suggesting no new official NOTAM/recognition dispute and varied airport exception lists; strongest direct page is an aggregator, not official Iranian source. Need official AIS/NOTAM source or authenticated retrieval and predecessor NOTAMs A0796/A0799 if evidence opens. Need airport-code mapping and operations/cancellation data for listed airports if two-airport complete suspension is alleged.

Filing plan: defendant opening should focus on burden and admitted-record gap. It can acknowledge likely dispute over an alleged OIIX A1010/26 but should not cite unadmitted web leads as proof. Argue council should require official/corroborated source chain, exact NOTAM text, active times converted to deadline, geographic scope, exceptions/permissions, commercial operation impact, and whether rule examples/airport path are actually met. Durable notes written to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Arguments opportunity. Re-scanned record and evidence after submissions. Submitted and accepted: ev_fa9c3b8e9519 (Indian Express extract reporting Iranian CAA NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 May 22-May 25 closing western Tehran FIR/airports, only eight airports operational with daylight-only and fresh CAA approval, including IKA/THR/IFN/SYZ/MHD); ev_204bcaab06e5 (Abdi Media extract reporting NOTAM A1010/26, all airports in western Tehran FIR closed, limited specified airports only sunrise-sunset, previous permissions canceled/new CAA authorization required for IFR passenger flights, valid May 22 00:16 Iran time to May 25 noon Iran time, security/military control context); ev_5d2bb5a5e96e (OPSGROUP context: western Iran remains heavily restricted, many western airports closed/daytime-only, east Tehran FIR only partial recovery-period reopening above FL285, west part remains closed).

Adverse/source-chain audit: I did not find a raw official NOTAM A1010/26 during time. Evidence is secondary but specific and corroborated by multiple sources; Indian Express attributes the NOTAM details to Gulf News/CAA, Abdi gives code/timing/operational restrictions, OPSGROUP independently supports ongoing western restrictions. Potential conflict: Indian Express and Abdi list different limited-operational airports; both agree these are exceptions/limited operations rather than normal operations. Better plaintiff theory is broad closure/suspension of western Tehran FIR or western Iranian airspace as a major region, with limited exceptions allowed by rule. Avoid overclaiming complete suspension at at least two listed airports because some listed airports appear to have limited daylight operations with new approvals. Deadline math: May 25 noon Iran time is after May 24 11:59 PM ET, so the restriction remained in force at market deadline. Non-weather basis supported by security/military-control context and strike/tension reporting, not weather.

Turn: arguments:defendant. Current record scanned with list_evidence/stat/read. Plaintiff added Indian Express ev_fa9c3b8e9519, Abdi Media ev_204bcaab06e5, OPSGROUP ev_5d2bb5a5e96e; exact contents read. All plaintiff source materials contain custody limits: Indian Express is a secondary report attributing to Gulf News/CAA; Abdi is secondary and no raw official NOTAM image/database record; OPSGROUP is aviation operations context, not Iranian authority. Defendant submitted ev_71e31604802f from Notamify display of OIIX A1010/26 raw NOTAM/interpretation. It shows raw displayed wording: Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999; A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST; all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW operational sunrise to sunset; previous permissions suspended; new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. Important caveat: Notamify is also an aggregator, not official Iranian custody. Defense argument plan: plaintiff has not carried preponderance because the rule’s preferred sources are official Iranian aviation authorities plus credible reporting consensus, but no official NOTAM/AIS record or predecessor A0796/A0799 is in record. Scope remains ambiguous: raw displayed text is airport-operations closure, not explicit closure of all commercial transits/arrivals/departures across a major airspace region; permitted civil IFR passenger operations continue at listed airports sunrise-to-sunset with permission. Plaintiff’s sources conflict on which eight airports remained operational. Indian Express lists IKA/THR/IFN/SYZ/MHD etc., while Abdi/Notamify list OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW; this defeats the two-listed-airport complete-suspension alternative and weakens source reliability. OPSGROUP confirms partial/recovery state with some overflights possible via temporary routes and eastern Tehran FIR reopening, not the exact May 24 closure. Do not overclaim because rule allows a major region and limited exceptions; argue burden remains unmet due to provenance and scope. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Rebuttal opportunity. Re-scanned evidence list and read defendant evidence ev_71e31604802f in full. D-1/Notamify raw display says A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26; Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999; A)OIIX; B)2605222046 C)2605250830 EST; E) REF NOTAM A0796/26, ALL AIRPORTS IN WEST PART OF TEHRAN FIR ARE CLSD except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW operational sunrise-to-sunset; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new permission must be requested from CAA for operation of civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. D-1 has aggregator provenance limits but corroborates plaintiff source chain and exact closure-plus-exceptions structure.

Rebuttal plan: answer defense source-quality/scope points. Concede raw official custody still not perfect, but D-1 supplied by defense strengthens preponderance by confirming same NOTAM code, date range, predecessor chain, CAA permission requirement, and raw phrase that all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed. Argue airport-operation wording still satisfies rule's broad closure/cancellation/suspension path because it covers commercial arrivals/departures in a major region and the rule expressly permits limited exceptions. Do not overclaim airport-specific alternative. Emphasize exception-list discrepancies are immaterial to broad-region theory: all versions agree on closure of west part/western Tehran FIR with narrow daylight/approval exceptions.

Turn: surrebuttals:defendant. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence. No new submitted evidence after arguments; plaintiff rebuttal re-offered defendant Notamify capture ev_71e31604802f as PX-R1 and argued D-1 strengthens plaintiff. Rebuttal's strongest point: D-1 displays raw text saying all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed daylight/permission operations, and plaintiff says that is a broad major-region closure with permissible exceptions. Surrebuttal plan: answer narrowly. D-1 does not cure source custody because it is still an aggregator and not official Iranian AIS/NOTAM record; missing predecessor A0796/A0799 remains. D-1's wording is narrower than plaintiff's gloss: page title/interpretation is airport operations; raw text says all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed airports, and only previous permissions/new CAA permissions for civil IFR passenger flights. It does not expressly say all commercial transit through western Tehran FIR is closed. Rule allows limited exceptions after proof of a broad closure; plaintiff cannot use exceptions/permission regime as substitute for proving broad transit/arrival/departure suspension. Source conflict remains: Indian Express exception list differs materially from Abdi/D-1; airport-specific path remains unproved. No new outside evidence needed; use current record.

Closing opportunity. Final record scan completed with get_case/list_evidence/stat. Evidence: market packet; plaintiff Indian Express, Abdi Media, OPSGROUP; defendant Notamify raw display. Defendant surrebuttal argues custody gap remains, wording is airport-operations not airspace transit closure, and exception-list conflicts undermine reliability. Closing plan: preponderance standard, not perfect official custody. Emphasize convergence of sources and D-1 raw display: A1010/26, OIIX/Tehran FIR, May 22-May 25 covering deadline, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed, prior permissions suspended for all operators, new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights, security/non-weather context. Concede official AIS/CAA original missing and listed-airport alternative not strongest. Argue first rule path: broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial aviation in a major Iranian airspace region; limited exceptions are allowed by the rule and D-1's daylight/new-permission exceptions do not defeat qualification.

Turn: closings:defendant. Record and final prompt scanned. Plaintiff closing argues D-1 corroborates NOTAM A1010/26 and that closure of western Tehran FIR airports plus permission exceptions qualifies as a broad regional commercial aviation suspension. Closing plan: concede relevant restriction/disruption but focus on burden and market rule. Decisive defense points: no official Iranian AIS/CAA NOTAM record; D-1 is still an aggregator with custody caveat; predecessor A0796/A0799 missing. Exact D-1 wording is airport-operations closure/permission regime, not express commercial transit closure across airspace. Rule allows limited exceptions only after broad closure is proven; here daylight and new-permission civil IFR passenger operations are central to the text. OPSGROUP shows partial recovery/complex operations with some overflights possible and eastern FIR reopened, not clean major closure. Exception lists conflict between PX-2 and PX-3/D-1, undermining reliability and airport-specific alternative. Plaintiff does not prove complete suspension at two listed airports. Ask council to find proposition not proven by preponderance. Journal updated.
